content:
- Completed the primary/replicas connection-helper task end to end: new CLI commands, shared sampled-cluster snapshot, DSN rendering, TLS path provenance, docs, binary tests, and one live HA switchover e2e scenario.
- Rebuilt docs and passed the required gates: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.
- Ran .ralph/task_switch.sh after setting the task file to completed with <passes>true</passes>; next step is only to commit all changes and push.

- Started Choose-Task turn.
- Read progress log and located .ralph/current_tasks.md plus .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Next: inspect active tasks, resolve blocked_by dependencies against current_tasks_done, then write the selected task path into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Inspected all active candidate task files directly.
- Found no active bug tasks and no blocked_by tags on the active candidates.
- Selected the next task by precedence and codebase logic: the medium-priority ctl debug/reporting task should precede the doc rewrite task because it creates the CLI surface that docs should teach.
- Next: write .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/05-task-add-debug-reporting-and-incident-surfaces-to-ctl.md into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Read prior progress and current task pointer.
- Active task is .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/05-task-add-debug-reporting-and-incident-surfaces-to-ctl.md.
- Next I am reading the task file to determine whether this turn is the planning, verification, or execution phase.

- Determined that the active task file has neither TO BE VERIFIED nor NOW EXECUTE.
- This turn is therefore the planning pass only.
- I am scanning the CLI, runtime, and docs for existing debug surfaces and status rendering so I can write a concrete execution plan into the task file.

- Read CLI args/status/output/client implementation.
- Found that status -v currently fetches only a tiny DebugVerboseResponse projection (pginfo summary + process state) and silently treats most debug fetch failures as absent data.
- Next I am reading the CLI dispatch path and the actual debug snapshot/view structs so the task plan can name the required refactors and error-handling changes explicitly.

- Read the requested docs first: three how-to guides plus debug API and pgtm CLI reference.
- Current docs posture is strongly API-first. The how-to guides repeatedly require curl/jq for /ha/state and /debug/verbose, while pgtm only covers status/primary/replicas/switchover.
- Next I am reading src/debug_api/*.rs and src/api/worker.rs to confirm the real verbose schema, query behavior, and whether any archive/report command or endpoint is already hinted in code rather than docs.

- Confirmed the runtime contract for /debug/verbose from src/debug_api/view.rs and src/api/worker.rs. The stable payload is explicit in code, schema_version is v1, and only changes/timeline are filtered by since.
- Confirmed there is no first-class debug/report/archive route in the API worker. /debug/snapshot, /debug/verbose, and /debug/ui are the only debug routes there.
- Also checked CLI internals: there is a get_debug_verbose client method used to enrich pgtm status -v, but the documented CLI command set still lacks any separate debug/report/archive command. Next I am pulling exact line references and then I can summarize rewrite impact by doc.

- Inspected src/cli/status.rs, src/cli/client.rs, src/cli/args.rs, src/cli/output.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/error.rs, tests/cli_binary.rs, and relevant api debug route tests.
- Confirmed existing debug surface is already wired into pgtm status via the global -v flag: build_sampled_cluster_snapshot fetches /ha/state, then optional /debug/verbose, and assemble/build_node_row maps leader/decision/pginfo/readiness/process into ClusterStatusView nodes.
- Confirmed renderer shape: human status output adds LEADER/DECISION/PGINFO/READINESS/PROCESS columns when view.verbose is true; JSON output serializes the whole ClusterStatusView including warnings, queried_via, switchover, nodes, and per-node observation_error.
- Important current behavior: fetch_optional_debug suppresses all /debug/verbose failures. 404 and 503 are explicitly mapped to None; every other error (including 401/403 auth and transport failures) is also dropped to None. So status -v still succeeds and shows ? / null debug fields, with no explicit warning for debug-disabled/auth/transport on the debug endpoint.
- Distinction to mention: /ha/state failures are not swallowed. Seed /ha/state transport errors fail the whole command with stderr containing transport error and exit code 3. Peer /ha/state failures degrade the cluster view via warnings plus API down/missing and observation_error in JSON, but the human table only shows warning lines and API column state.
- Existing test seams: cli output unit tests for compact vs verbose table and JSON rendering; cli status unit tests for degraded view assembly and verbose mode without debug payload; cli client tests for auth header selection and ApiStatus/Decode/Transport mapping; cli_binary tests for human/json status rendering and top-level transport/config failures. No existing binary test directly exercises status -v against /debug/verbose.

- Completed the planning pass for .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/05-task-add-debug-reporting-and-incident-surfaces-to-ctl.md.
- Wrote a detailed execution plan into the task file and ended it with TO BE VERIFIED as instructed.
- Key planning decisions captured: keep routine investigation in `pgtm status -v`, add a shallow `pgtm debug` surface for raw verbose retrieval/since/report workflows, replace silent debug failure swallowing with explicit per-node debug availability states, and update the curl-heavy docs to lead with the CLI.
- Subagent findings confirmed the main gaps: no standalone CLI debug/report command today, `/debug/verbose` exposes a stable full payload worth modeling directly, and monitor-via-metrics/debug-cluster-issues/handle-network-partition are the heaviest doc rewrites.
- Next engineer should do the skeptical verification pass, alter at least one thing in the plan, replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE only when satisfied, and then execute without re-exploring.

- Loaded previous progress and current task pointer.
- Current task still points to .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/05-task-add-debug-reporting-and-incident-surfaces-to-ctl.md.
- Prior notes indicate the task file was left in TO BE VERIFIED state; next step is to read the live task file and do the skeptical review if that marker is still present.

- Read the task file in TO BE VERIFIED state and completed the required skeptical review.
- Altered the plan materially before approving execution: narrowed the must-have scope to status -v plus pgtm debug verbose, made debug report optional/deferred unless truly small, preserved the current default-to-status CLI parsing shape instead of forcing a broad args refactor, switched the plan to reuse/extract the existing debug verbose schema instead of duplicating it in cli-only structs, and corrected docs strategy so debug-api.md stays API-first while CLI-first guidance moves into how-to/tutorial docs.
- Also added docs review coverage for tutorial/observing-failover.md because it still teaches raw debug snapshot polling.
- Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE in the task file. Next engineer should execute the plan as written without re-exploring, tick boxes as work completes, and then run the full required make check/test/test-long/lint gates.

- Read previous progress log and current task pointer.
- Current task points to .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/05-task-add-debug-reporting-and-incident-surfaces-to-ctl.md.
- Need to inspect the live task file because prior progress says it was moved to NOW EXECUTE, which changes whether I should plan, review, or execute this turn.

- Confirmed the task file is already in NOW EXECUTE state, so I moved directly into implementation.
- Inspected src/cli/args.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/client.rs, src/cli/status.rs, and src/cli/output.rs.
- Current gaps match the plan: no debug command tree, debug client only models pginfo/process, and status verbose silently drops most debug endpoint failures.
- Next I am aligning the CLI with the shared debug verbose schema and the existing binary test harness before patching code.

- Read the shared debug API view structs and confirmed they are serialize-only, with borrowed/static string fields that are unsuitable for CLI deserialization.
- Read more of status/output/tests and confirmed the current rendering and binary harness are straightforward to extend.
- Minor note: there is no src/main.rs entrypoint for the CLI binary, so I am locating the actual pgtm binary target before patching dispatch.

- Core implementation now includes a new `pgtm debug verbose` command path, shared owned `/debug/verbose` payload structs, and explicit debug observation states in verbose cluster status.
- Added initial unit and binary tests for debug payload decoding, `--since` query emission, verbose status detail rendering, and auth/disabled behaviors.
- I am running targeted test suites now before moving on to the docs rewrites and full make gates.

- Finished the code implementation and the targeted CLI/unit/binary test pass.
- Rewrote the required how-to/tutorial docs directly to make the new flows CLI-first and restored the accidentally deleted debug-cluster-issues guide.
- Next I am running docs-build and then the full required make gates: check, test, test-long, lint.

- All required verification passed in this execution pass: docs-build, make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.
- Next I am updating the task file checkboxes and passes flag, then I will run .ralph/task_switch.sh, commit all changes including .ralph, and push.
